trade.summary: Canada exports natural resources, aerospace products, and agricultural goods to Svizzera. Svizzera exports pharmaceuticals, precision instruments, watches, and financial services. Both are high-income economies with complementary advanced sectors.

diplomatic.summary: Strong diplomatic relations between two stable, high-income democracies. Canada and Svizzera share values of multilateralism, rule of law, and human rights. Both engage constructively in OECD, WTO, and other multilateral forums.

military.summary: No formal military alliance. Canada is NATO member; Svizzera is neutral (though recently increased NATO coordination). Very limited bilateral military engagement.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment flows. Swiss banks (UBS, Credit Suisse) have had Canadian operations. Swiss pharmaceutical companies (Roche, Novartis) operate in Canada. Canadian pension funds invest substantially in Swiss assets.

energy.summary: Very limited direct energy trade. Canada does not significantly export fossil fuels to Svizzera. Some cooperation on clean energy technology.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation. Canadian and Swiss universities collaborate extensively in physics (CERN-Canada links), life sciences, and environmental research. ETH Zurich has partnerships with several Canadian universities.
